care.summary: A striking, low-maintenance houseplant with slender, arching leaves edged in red.
care.light: Bright indirect to medium light; tolerates lower light but grows slower
care.water: Water when top half of soil is dry; sensitive to overwatering
care.soil: Well-draining potting mix with sand or perlite
care.humidity: Low to average; tolerates dry indoor air well
care.fertilizer: Dilute balanced fertilizer every 6-8 weeks spring through summer
common_issues: Brown leaf tips from fluoride or chemicals in tap water
Root rot in poorly draining or soggy soil
Yellow lower leaves — natural aging, or a sign of overwatering
Spider mites in dry, dusty conditions
tips: Use distilled or rainwater to prevent leaf tip browning
Prune canes to control height and encourage branching
Dust leaves regularly to keep photosynthesis efficient
Let soil dry more in winter months
